label: independent
hint_value: Make the sides, the front and the back of the bus by drawing a cartoon design on a plywood sheet and cutting them out. Attach the sides to the front and the back using nails and screws to form a box in the shape of a bus.

The key difference is whether the bottom is made from the same plywood sheet as the sides, front, and back. If you're building a box shape, the bottom is essential. If you cut the sides, front, and bottom from the same sheet, maybe you can't form all parts correctly. Typically, a bed box would have four sides (left and right), front, back, and a bottom. So to make a box, you'd need the sides to be connected to the front and back, and the bottom would be another piece. If the bottom is part of the same sheet, maybe it's not cut out as a separate part. Alternatively, maybe the bottom is a separate piece of plywood. But the question is about the answer choices. 

The Stanford professor chose B. Let me check the wording again. Option A mentions making the sides, front, and bottom by drawing a cartoon design on a plywood sheet and cutting them out. But if you're making a bus-shaped bed, the bottom would be the base, but how is that part of the cartoon design? The cartoon design would be on the sides and front/back. question_text: To build a toddler bed that looks like a bus.

choices.A: Make the sides, the front and the bottom of the bus by drawing a cartoon design on a plywood sheet and cutting them out. Attach the sides to the front and the back using nails and screws to form a box in the shape of a bus.
choices.B: Make the sides, the front and the back of the bus by drawing a cartoon design on a plywood sheet and cutting them out. Attach the sides to the front and the back using nails and screws to form a box in the shape of a bus.
